authoritySource: Dr. Ahmed Hesham — OET Grammar Rulebook for Nurses v1 (inherits medicine)

rules.title: Shift events in past simple
rules.body: Describe handover events in the past simple: 'The patient was repositioned at 14:00.'

rules.title: Ongoing care in present simple
rules.body: Use present simple for ongoing nursing care that continues at the time of writing: 'She requires two-hourly repositioning.'

rules.title: Since + starting point = present perfect
rules.body: Nursing notes that mention 'since' the admission / since the fall / since the last dose require present perfect.
exemplarPhrases: She has been nil by mouth since midnight.

rules.title: For + duration = present perfect
rules.body: Nursing notes with 'for [duration]' require present perfect.
exemplarPhrases: She has been afebrile for 24 hours.

rules.title: X ago = past simple
rules.body: Time markers with 'ago' use past simple.
exemplarPhrases: The wound dressing was changed two hours ago.

rules.title: Handover uses passive voice
rules.body: Nursing handover uses passive voice for clinical actions when the performer is implicit: 'IV fluids were commenced.'

rules.title: Reverse rule with 'along with'
rules.body: 'Along with', 'together with', 'as well as' do NOT change subject number.
exemplarPhrases: The patient, along with family members, was informed of the plan.

rules.title: No contractions
rules.body: Never use contractions in professional nursing letters or handovers.

rules.title: Never use 'the patient' repetitively
rules.body: Refer to the patient by title + surname; avoid 'the patient' as a running label.

rules.title: Care FOR not FROM
rules.body: Use 'treatment for', 'admitted for', 'referred for'. Never 'admitted from the ward' to mean 'admitted to the ward for'.

rules.title: Translate Latin abbreviations
rules.body: Translate Latin dose abbreviations (od/bd/tds/qds/stat/prn) into plain English in formal letters.

rules.title: Observations with units
rules.body: All observations carry units: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 72 bpm, SpO₂ 98%, Temp 36.8 °C.
